name: Jellied Cottage Cheese-Fruit Salad (Usda)
recipeIngredient: 40 ounces canned crushed pineapple, drained, 2 (20-oz) cans
recipeInstructions:
1. Line four 8x8x2-inch baking pans with heat-resistant freezer wrap. Allow enough extra wrap to fold over the top. Use one pan for each six servings or one-fourth of the recipe. Do not line pans for food that will be served without freezing.
2. Dissolve gelatin in boiling water. Stir in fruit liquid. Stir in cottage cheese and fruits.
3. Pour one-fourth of the mixture into each pan. Chill until firm.
To Serve Without Freezing:
Serve on crisp salad greens.
To Freeze:
1. Complete wrapping by pulling paper up over the top of the food. Put edges of wrap together and fold several times so paper lies directly on top of the food.
2. Fold ends of freezer wrap over the top and seal with freezer tape. Label with the name of the food, date of freezing, and last date the food should be used for best eating quality (about 6 months).
3. Freeze immediately for 10 to 12 hours before removing packages from the pans.
To Thaw Frozen Salads:
1. Remove freezer wrap.
2. Place food in a baking pan.
3. Thaw in the refrigerator for about 24 hours. Serve on crisp salad greens.
